script_user_append: Make the first video feel like one believable outing with three clearly different stages: doorway start, enclosed garden middle, open-air arrival. Keep the same rider, spouse, wardrobe, wheelchair, and screen direction throughout. Avoid direct salesy overlays in the generated footage and avoid repeated backgrounds.
scene_pic_user_append: Keep the couple anonymous and non-testimonial. No direct eye contact to camera, no face-led smile-to-camera hero portrait, no close-up portrait framing, and no crisp celebrity-like facial detail. Use medium or medium-wide lifestyle framing with soft side profiles or half profiles. Scene 1 must visibly include the doorway or threshold. Scene 2 must feel deeper into the garden, with closer shrubs or flowers and less visible house. Scene 3 must feel more open, with more sky, more sidewalk cues, and less dense greenery than scene 2.
video_prompt_append: Keep the couple anonymous and non-testimonial. Avoid close-up portrait energy, direct eye contact to camera, and face-led hero framing. Prefer medium or medium-wide lifestyle composition, soft side profiles, and naturally motivated movement. The three scenes must feel continuous from shot to shot but clearly different in environment density: start near the doorway, move through a more enclosed garden middle, then arrive in a more open sidewalk space with more sky and less nearby greenery.
